is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study of 69 Kenyan Plasmodium falciparum field isolates and their DHFR genotypes.

reports_target_markers.evidence: Reports Pfdhfr codons 51, 59, and 108 (target markers), plus codon 164.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Frequencies given: 'alleles that carried a mutation at codon 59 (61%) were more frequent than those that carried a mutation at codon 51 (35%)'; 'only about 8% of Kenyan field isolates now have the wild-type DHFR'; '92% of isolates resistant.' Tables give n per genotype group.

sub_country_location.evidence: Field isolates collected in Kilifi, on the Kenyan coast — a sub-national town/site location.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: Isolates collected between 1993 and 1995 in Kilifi — a 3-year window (<=3y).

african_field_samples.evidence: Kenyan P. falciparum field isolates collected from patients during a clinical trial in Kilifi, Kenya.

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary empirical study: in vitro chemosensitivity testing and PCR genotyping of collected field isolates.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Field isolates were collected during a clinical trial of CPG-DDS in Kilifi (Amukoye et al. 1997, ref 1); samples derive from that named trial and may overlap with other publications from the same collection.

supplement.evidence: Frequency data (percentages and n per genotype group) are present in the main text and Tables 1-3; no supplementary tables referenced.

assessment.summary: Study of 69 Kenyan P. falciparum field isolates from Kilifi (collected 1993-1995) correlating DHFR genotypes at codons 51, 59, 108 (and 164) with in vitro antifolate sensitivity. Reports mutation frequencies (codon 59: 61%, codon 51: 35%, wild-type DHFR: ~8%) tied to a single sub-national site (Kilifi) within a 3-year window. All criteria appear met, though frequencies are only per-genotype-group (single-allele subset of 32/69) rather than clean per-codon numerator/denominator, and samples derive from a named clinical trial.
